trial_label: Palbociclib in Combination With Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy f

rubric.target_patient: Adult men and pre/postmenopausal women with histologically confirmed HR+/HER2-negative Stage II (except T2N0) or Stage III invasive breast cancer, in the adjuvant setting, who have already demonstrated tolerance to at least one month of tamoxifen or an aromatase inhibitor and have a plan for at least 2 years of continued endocrine therapy. Patients may have received prior (neo)adjuvant chemotherapy or radiotherapy but must be past acute toxicity. This is a curative-intent, early-stage adjuvant population, not metastatic disease.
rubric.site_burden: Based on the Schedule of Assessments (weak evidence — heading matched but page not a structured grid), the protocol involves monthly visits for the first 4 cycles, then every 8 weeks through Cycle 12, then every 12 weeks through Year 2 (24 cycles total, ~2 years), with serial CBC with differential (including Day 15 draws in early cycles), ECGs at multiple timepoints for QTc monitoring, drug diaries and pill counts at every visit, and paper-based QOL surveys (EORTC QLQ-C30) at defined intervals. Sites need oncology infusion/clinic infrastructure capable of frequent labs and ECGs, on-site or networked lab capability with fax-back result review before dosing, research staff for drug diary/pill count adherence tracking, and biospecimen handling/shipping (including a defined dry-ice/cryovial workaround for Friday/holiday draws) coordinated through a central CaTissue tracking system.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist or breast cancer specialist qualified to manage adjuvant HR+/HER2- breast cancer treatment decisions, including endocrine therapy and CDK4/6 inhibitor toxicity management.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists the physician as Medical Oncology, Hematology/Oncology, or Internal Medicine with an oncology subspecialty designation.
criteria.derived_from: Interventions: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) + Aromatase Inhibitor/Endocrine Therapy for HR+/HER2- invasive breast cancer

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have demonstrated clinical or research familiarity with HR+/HER2-negative breast cancer management, given the trial's focus on endocrine-therapy-based adjuvant treatment.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed search returns at least 1 publication by the physician on hormone receptor positive breast cancer, endocrine therapy, or CDK4/6 inhibitors.
criteria.derived_from: Title: 'Palbociclib in Combination With Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy for Hormone Receptor Positive, HER2 Negative Invasive Breast Cancer'

criteria.requirement: The site must see a regular volume of Stage II-III HR+/HER2-negative breast cancer patients in the adjuvant setting to support feasible enrollment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare claims data show the physician bills for a floor volume of breast cancer diagnosis/treatment E&M or infusion codes consistent with an active breast oncology practice (or physician self-attests to monthly volume of new adjuvant HR+ breast cancer patients).
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: HR+ HER2 negative Stage II (except T2N0) or Stage III invasive breast cancer

criteria.requirement: The practice must have a pipeline of patients already on or recently started on tamoxifen/AI therapy who have tolerated at least one month without significant adverse events, since enrollment requires prior demonstrated endocrine tolerance.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-attests that their practice routinely initiates and monitors adjuvant endocrine therapy in HR+ breast cancer patients as standard of care.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: demonstrated ability to tolerate endocrine therapy by prior successful completion of at least 1 month of tamoxifen or AI

criteria.requirement: Since the trial enrolls both men and women, the site should have at least occasional experience managing male breast cancer patients or be prepared to do so.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-attests to having evaluated or treated at least one male breast cancer patient, or practice is a comprehensive cancer center handling rare presentations.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: Men and both pre- and postmenopausal women are eligible

criteria.requirement: The site must be able to perform serial CBC with differential and 12-lead ECG monitoring on a recurring multi-year visit schedule (monthly initially, tapering to every 8-12 weeks over 2 years), including QTc assessment.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician/site self-attests to on-site or networked access to CBC lab draws and ECG equipment capable of supporting the described 2-year visit cadence with local result turnaround before dosing decisions.
criteria.derived_from: Operational Burden: monthly CBC/ECG monitoring cycles, Day 15 CBCs in early cycles, ECGs at Day 1 of multiple cycles through 2 years

criteria.requirement: The site must be equipped to process, aliquot, freeze (-80C), and ship research blood specimens on dry ice per the CaTissue-tracked coordinating center protocol.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician/site self-attests to having -80C freezer storage, dry ice shipping capability, and research staff trained in specimen aliquoting and CaTissue-linked sample tracking.
criteria.derived_from: Operational Burden: blood specimen aliquoting/freezing/shipping instructions and CaTissue tracking system

criteria.requirement: The site needs research staff to maintain drug diaries, perform pill counts, and administer paper QOL surveys at every visit across a 2-year treatment course.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician/site self-attests to dedicated research coordinator or nursing staff responsible for adherence tracking (pill counts, drug diaries) and QOL survey administration at scheduled visits.
criteria.derived_from: Operational Burden: drug diaries, pill counts each visit, EORTC QLQ-C30 QOL survey at defined cycle visits

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior experience serving as a site investigator on an interventional oncology trial, demonstrating capacity to run multi-year protocol-driven treatment and monitoring.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as Principal Investigator or Sub-Investigator on at least 1 completed or ongoing interventional oncology trial.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute (cooperative multi-site academic trial network, Phase 2)

criteria.requirement: Given palbociclib is an industry-developed CDK4/6 inhibitor, prior research relationships with pharmaceutical sponsors (e.g., Pfizer) suggest familiarity with sponsor-monitored trial conduct and GCP compliance.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments database shows at least 1 research payment record associated with the physician from a pharmaceutical company within the oncology space in the years surrounding trial conduct.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor), industry-originated compound used under academic-sponsor Phase 2 trial

criteria.requirement: Given the trial ran through a Dana-Farber-affiliated multi-site network (DF/HCC), the investigator should be affiliated with an academic cancer center or a network site linked to a major cooperative group.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES practice address matches one of the trial's listed site institutions or an affiliated satellite/community site within the same health system.
criteria.derived_from: Site Footprint: DFCI-affiliated network sites (DF/DWCC Milford, MGH/North Shore, South Shore Hospital) alongside academic centers (MGH, BIDMC, UCSF, Indiana University)

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status 0-1 — a per-patient fitness threshold checked at enrollment, not a property of the physician or site.
Baseline QTc ≤480 ms — a per-patient cardiac safety cutoff assessed at screening.
Contraception requirement for women who might become pregnant — individual patient counseling/compliance item, not physician-fit.
Ability to understand and willingness to sign informed consent — assessed per patient at enrollment.
Pregnancy/breastfeeding exclusion — per-patient eligibility check, not a site capability.
History of prior malignancy exclusions (5-year disease-free rule with carve-outs) — individual patient oncologic history reviewed case-by-case.
Timing since last chemo/radiotherapy dose and residual toxicity grade — per-patient washout/toxicity assessment at screening.

Prior demonstrated tolerance of 1 month of endocrine therapy — a per-patient clinical history check, not a site characteristic (though the population-level analog is captured separately as pipeline capacity).
Exclusion of prior CDK4/6 inhibitor use — per-patient treatment history check at screening.
